name: Read-write lock with writer-preference
task: Create /tmp/eval_rwlock/rwl.py with class RWLock. Methods: acquire_read() (blocks while a writer holds the lock OR a writer is waiting), release_read(), acquire_write() (blocks while any reader holds the lock or another writer holds the lock), release_write(). Writer preference: once a writer is queued, NEW readers must block until that writer has acquired and released, even if other readers are currently reading. Multiple concurrent readers are allowed when no writer is holding/waiting. Implement using threading.Lock and threading.Condition only — do NOT import or use threading.RLock or any third-party rwlock.

validation.script:
import importlib.util, threading, time; spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('m', '/tmp/eval_rwlock/rwl.py'); mod = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec); spec.loader.exec_module(mod); src = open('/tmp/eval_rwlock/rwl.py').read(); assert 'threading.RLock' not in src and 'from threading import RLock' not in src, 'must not use RLock'
# Multiple readers concurrent
lock = mod.RWLock(); concurrent = [0]; max_concurrent = [0]; clk = threading.Lock()
def reader_count():
    lock.acquire_read()
    with clk:
        concurrent[0] += 1
        max_concurrent[0] = max(max_concurrent[0], concurrent[0])
    time.sleep(0.05)
    with clk: concurrent[0] -= 1
    lock.release_read()
ths = [threading.Thread(target=reader_count) for _ in range(5)]
for t in ths: t.start()
for t in ths: t.join()
assert max_concurrent[0] >= 3, f'readers should be concurrent, max was {max_concurrent[0]}'
# Writer-preference test
lock = mod.RWLock(); events = []; ev_lk = threading.Lock()
def rec(tag):
    with ev_lk: events.append((tag, time.monotonic()))
lock.acquire_read(); rec('R1_acquired')
w_done = threading.Event()
def writer():
    rec('W_request')
    lock.acquire_write(); rec('W_acquired')
    time.sleep(0.05)
    lock.release_write(); rec('W_released'); w_done.set()
wt = threading.Thread(target=writer); wt.start()
time.sleep(0.1)  # let writer queue up
r2_done = threading.Event()
def late_reader():
    rec('R2_request')
    lock.acquire_read(); rec('R2_acquired')
    lock.release_read(); rec('R2_released'); r2_done.set()
rt = threading.Thread(target=late_reader); rt.start()
time.sleep(0.1)
# At this point: R1 holds, W queued, R2 should be queued behind W (writer preference)
tags = [t for t,_ in events]
assert 'W_acquired' not in tags, f'writer got lock while reader holds: {tags}'
assert 'R2_acquired' not in tags, f'late reader got lock while writer queued (no writer-preference): {tags}'
lock.release_read(); rec('R1_released')
wt.join(timeout=2); rt.join(timeout=2)
assert w_done.is_set() and r2_done.is_set(), 'writer or late-reader stuck'
tags = [t for t,_ in events]
w_idx = tags.index('W_acquired'); r2_idx = tags.index('R2_acquired')
assert w_idx < r2_idx, f'writer must finish before late reader runs (writer-pref): {tags}'
assert tags.index('W_released') < r2_idx, f'late reader ran while writer held: {tags}'
print('OK')
